Environmental Impact Statement, Center Leg, Inner Loop Freeway, H Street and Massachusetts Avenue to New York Avenue

The Environmental Impact Statement discusses the proposed extension of the Center Leg of the Inner Loop Freeway (I-95) in Washington, D.C., from Massachusetts Avenue to New York Avenue. The project aims to improve traffic flow and reduce congestion by providing a more efficient route for vehicles, which is expected to alleviate air pollution issues caused by traffic jams. The extension is also intended to support mass transit by facilitating express bus lanes. The project is part of a broader effort to meet air quality standards and improve transportation infrastructure in the region.
Washington, D.C., from Massachusetts Avenue to New York Avenue
District of Columbia Department of Highways and Traffic

The proposed extension of the Center Leg of the Inner Loop Freeway was part of a larger initiative to enhance traffic flow and reduce congestion in Washington, D.C. The project aimed to address air pollution concerns linked to traffic jams while also supporting mass transit options. However, it faced public pushback from community groups and environmental advocates who were concerned about the potential negative impacts on neighborhoods and the environment. Ultimately, the project was not pursued as planned, reflecting the complexities of balancing infrastructure development with community and environmental considerations.
"There is already voluminous testimony adverse to this route in the records of the District of Columbia City Council." — Arthur J. Reid, Jr., Director, Intergovernmental Relations

"The extension will not induce increased vehicle miles of travel but rather will provide a more efficient terminus for traffic control and thereby avoid the pollution effects of inefficient intersection design." — Mr. Daniel S. Hammond, Federal Highway Administration
